provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
This Order may be cited as the Communications Regulatory Authority (Designation of Public Postal Operator) Order.

provisions.title: Designation of Public Postal Operator
provisions.content:
2. Designation of Public Postal Operator
Botswana Postal Services is hereby designated as a Public Postal Operator from 1st April, 2014 to 31st March, 2019.

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Regulations may be cited as the Communications Regulatory Authority Regulations.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Classification of broadcasting licences

definitions.definition: the broadcasting or distribution, by any means, of any item or information which is intended to promote a cause or to inform or influence its recipients, in return for payment or other valuable consideration
the centre of the frequency band assigned to a broadcasting service
any person who composes or distributes broadcasting service for reception by subscribers to such services or the general public
a licence issued in terms of section 32 of the Act for the provision of a broadcasting service
the date on which a licensee commences the operation of a regulated sector
a licence that enables the holder to broadcast audio or data services such as text to a subscriber or to the general public
a licence that enables the holder to broadcast video, audio or data services such as text to a subscriber or to the general public
the territory covered by a broadcasting station signal strength, allowing for clear reception, and includes an area specified in a broadcasting licence by the Board
to disable a telecommunications service to a subscriber by a licensee

a procedure by which a manufacturer or a supplier gives a written assurance in accordance with International Standard Organisation (ISO) or International Electro-technical Commission (IEC) standards that Radio and Telecommunications Terminal Equipment (RTTE) conforms to these Regulations
any event or circumstance arising out of accidents, natural phenomena, fires, floods, explosions, or incidents involving exposure or potential exposure to radioactive or toxic materials
the period immediately following the announcement of an election date until the close of polling, in accordance with the Electoral Act (Cap. 02:09)
an output of another broadcaster broadcasting live as a licensee&rsquo
any interference which endangers the functioning of a radio service, or which degrades, obstructs or repeatedly interrupts such a radio service operating in accordance with the assigned frequency

an electronic-based postal service whereby a sender posts the original message in either a physical or an electronic form, which is then electronically processed and converted into a letter-post item for physical delivery to the addressee
any details given by a subscriber to a licensee or a licensee&rsquo

any advertising broadcast in visual or audio form, lasting for more than two minutes, which may contain demonstrations of the use of the product or service advertised, entailing direct offers to the public in return for payment, and which results in the broadcaster receiving payment in monetary terms or otherwise

the effect of unwanted energy due to one or a combination of emissions, radiations or indications upon reception in a radio system, manifested by any performance degradation, misinterpretation or loss of information which could be extracted in the absence of such unwanted energy
any form of written communication or personal correspondence, including a postcard
a service provider licenced under the Act by the Authority
broadcasting of the scheduled real time content that is received by a subscriber at the time of broadcast
the output or results of a regulated sectors&rsquo
a coverage area of a terrestrial broadcasting transmitter confined to locality within a town or village and does not exceed a radius of three kilometres from the transmitter
a coverage area of the whole country
a part of the numbering plan&rsquo

a system that allows the viewer to block out a channel or programming using an exclusive access control technology such as personal identification number (PIN) in order to select an appropriate level of suitable programming
a party which is either lawfully registered as a political party in Botswana
a system for the collection, dispatch, holding and delivery of postal articles by or through public postal licensee
any building, house, room, vehicle or place, where postal services are offered
as "postal item" and shall include an insured postal item
any charges imposed or levied by the licensee for the provision of postal services
a series of digits or alphabets or a unique identifier appended to a postal address identifying a locality
any service provided by a licensee exclusively, or in conjunction with any other person, by means of which emergency broadcasts are made free of charge
a licence issued in terms of section 45 of the Act
a coverage area of a terrestrial broadcasting transmitter intended to cover a town or village or a group of people of towns and villages which does not exceed a radius of 50 kilometres
the recording of identification particulars of a subscriber by a licensee or a licensee&rsquo
a subscription management service referred to in regulation 3(1)( c )
any signs, sounds, text, images, information or data of any configuration sent, or to be sent, for conveyance by a system operated by a licensee

a broadcasting licence issued for an event that does not last for more than seven days or any other extended period as may be determined by the Authority and which is broadcast—
( a ) outside a licence&rsquo
a programme that has all or part of its costs paid by a sponsor, with a view to promoting that sponsor&rsquo
an arrangement of programmes which are presented on a broadcasting station
a person who uses or accesses a communication service provided by a licensee
to temporarily disable a regulated communications service to a subscriber by a licensee

any system of conveying signals, sounds, communications or other information through the agency of electric, magnetic, electro-magnetic, electro-chemical or electro-mechanical energy, or through the agency of radio waves
an item which, has not been claimed by an addressee
ascribed to it in section 2 of the Act
the specialised agency of the United Nations dealing with postal services
a period from 9.00 p.m. to 6.00 a.m. when material unsuitable for children may be broadcast.
